name: Iron Cross Stretch
shortDescription: A lying rotational stretch that improves spinal mobility and stretches the obliques and lower back.
instructions: Lie on your back with arms spread out in a 'T' position. Lift one leg straight up and sweep it across your body to the opposite side, letting it fall toward the floor while keeping your shoulders flat. Hold the position, feeling a stretch through your torso and hip.
steps: Lie on your back with arms extended out to the sides.
Lift one leg straight up towards the ceiling.
Sweep the raised leg across your body to the opposite side.
Allow your foot to touch or lower towards the floor.
Hold the stretch, then switch legs.
breathing: Exhale as you move into the twist, inhale as you return to center.
